task.title: Write a workflow for conditional rule chains
task.shortdesc: A workflow chains rules together so that some checks only run when others pass or fail. Write a workflow when your editorial requirement is conditional — it only applies in specific circumstances.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Write a markdown-validator workflow to chain rules with conditional logic, so checks only run when their preconditions are met.

ul.li: You have defined the individual rules the workflow will reference in your rule set file.
Review the workflow concept: Workflow language overview.
Review the step-pattern reference: Workflow step reference.

context.p: Before writing the workflow, clarify the conditional logic in plain English. For example: "If ms.topic is 'tutorial' (rule 1 passes), then also check that the H1 starts with 'Tutorial:' (rule 2). If it is not 'tutorial' (rule 1 fails), pass without checking rule 2."

step.cmd: Map the plain-English logic to the step token sequence.

info.p: For a simple conditional check (check rule B only when rule A passes):
info.codeblock: S-A, A-D, T-B, F-sentinel, B-M, sentinel-M, M-E
info.p: Where sentinel is the ID of a rule that always passes for valid documents (typically rule 1 in your rule set).
Example: check H1 format only when ms.topic == tutorial (rule 1) passes:

step.cmd: Add the workflow object to the workflows array in your rule set file.
Test the workflow against documents that cover both branches.

info.codeblock:
# Document where ms.topic = tutorial (should evaluate rule 6) md-validate validate tutorial-article.md --rules rules/tutorial.json
# Document where ms.topic != tutorial (should skip rule 6) md-validate validate how-to-article.md --rules rules/tutorial.json
info.p: For interactive workflow testing, see Test a workflow in the REPL.
